result.stdout:
Description:
  openf1-console

Usage:
  openf1-console [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose                      Whether verbose logging should be enabled [default: False]
  --with-api                         Whether the API endpoint should be exposed at http://localhost:61937 [default: False]
  --data-directory <data-directory>  The directory to which timing data will be read from and written to
  --notify                           Whether audible BELs are sent to your terminal when new race control messages are received
  --version                          Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

Commands:
  import <year>  Imports data from the F1 Live Timing API, if you have missed recording a session live. 
                 The data is imported in a format that can be replayed in real-time using openf1-console.

Description:
  openf1-console

Usage:
  openf1-console [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose                      Whether verbose logging should be enabled [default: False]
  --with-api                         Whether the API endpoint should be exposed at http://localhost:61937 [default: False]
  --data-directory <data-directory>  The directory to which timing data will be read from and written to
  --notify                           Whether audible BELs are sent to your terminal when new race control messages are received
  --version                          Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

Commands:

                 The data is imported in a format that can be replayed in real-time using openf1-console.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Imports data from the F1 Live Timing API, if you have missed recording a session live. 
  The data is imported in a format that can be replayed in real-time using openf1-console.

Usage:
  openf1-console import [<year>] [command] [options]

Arguments:
  <year>  The year the meeting took place.

Options:
  -m, --meeting, --meeting-key <meeting-key>  The Meeting Key of the session to import. If not provided, all meetings in the year will be listed.
  -s, --session, --session-key <session-key>  The Session Key of the session inside the selected meeting to import. If not provided, all sessions in the provided meeting will be listed.
  -v, --verbose                               Whether verbose logging should be enabled [default: False]
  --with-api                                  Whether the API endpoint should be exposed at http://localhost:61937 [default: False]
  --data-directory <data-directory>           The directory to which timing data will be read from and written to
  --notify                                    Whether audible BELs are sent to your terminal when new race control messages are received
  -?, -h, --help                              Show help and usage information


Commands:
  list <year>  [DEPRECATED - Use the import command directly without all options to list meetings and sessions]
               Lists the available Meetings and Sessions that can be imported.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Imports data from the F1 Live Timing API, if you have missed recording a session live. 
  The data is imported in a format that can be replayed in real-time using openf1-console.

Usage:
  openf1-console import [<year>] [command] [options]

Arguments:
  <year>  The year the meeting took place.

Options:
  -m, --meeting, --meeting-key <meeting-key>  The Meeting Key of the session to import. If not provided, all meetings in the year will be listed.
  -s, --session, --session-key <session-key>  The Session Key of the session inside the selected meeting to import. If not provided, all sessions in the provided meeting will be listed.
  -v, --verbose                               Whether verbose logging should be enabled [default: False]
  --with-api                                  Whether the API endpoint should be exposed at http://localhost:61937 [default: False]
  --data-directory <data-directory>           The directory to which timing data will be read from and written to
  --notify                                    Whether audible BELs are sent to your terminal when new race control messages are received
  -?, -h, --help                              Show help and usage information


Commands:
  list <year>  [DEPRECATED - Use the import command directly without all options to list meetings and sessions]
               Lists the available Meetings and Sessions that can be imported.
